authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Cold Creek Pond and Cold Creek: Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Cold Creek Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Stocking Updates: 996 Rainbow Trout, 8.8 in, 03-30-2026; 1008 Rainbow Trout, 8.9 in, 03-03-2026; 992 Rainbow Trout, 10.0 in, 11-10-2025; 1445 Rainbow Trout, 9.2 in, 03-17-2025; 987 Rainbow Trout, 9.6 in, 11-06-2024; 1000 Rainbow Trout, 9.4 in, 03-13-2024; 998 Rainbow Trout, 9.3 in, 11-06-2023; 1001 Rainbow Trout, 9.8 in, 04-19-2023; 1020 Rainbow Trout, 9 in, 04-06-2023.

sources.title: Cold Creek Pond — Fishery and Stocking Information (Angler Guide 2025) — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Cold Creek is stocked in the spring and then again in the fall with Rainbow Trout. 2024 Rainbow Trout Stocking: 3/13/2024, 1,000, 9.4 inches; 11/6/2024, 987, 9.6 inches.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 859284, "Cold Creek", Stream, Clark County, NV
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":859284,"gaz_name":"Cold Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Clark"} — geometry multipoint from Web Mercator (-12885822.3521, 4365789.6481) to (-12884183.4054, 4357832.8662), converting to approximately lon -115.755/lat 36.472 (upstream end) to lon -115.741/lat 36.415 (downstream end). Cold Creek Pond has no separate GNIS point record of its own; it is one of three small ponds fed by this creek reach (per NDOW's Cold Creek Pond Angler Guide). The corrected coordinate (-115.742, 36.4257) falls on this GNIS stream line, corroborated by NDOW's Angler Guide access directions (turn onto Shootingstar Road off Cold Creek Road, pass two smaller ponds, reach the larger Cold Creek Pond in about ½ mile) and by Clark County Fire Station 82's geocoded address on Cold Creek Rd near Camp Bonanza Rd (~36.4223, -115.7304, ~1.1 km away) — the file's previous coordinate (-115.7269, 36.4756) sat about 5.9 km north of the fire station and off the Cold Creek drainage entirely.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Clark County (Cold Creek Pond and Cold Creek)
regulations.humanSummary: Cold Creek Pond is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Cold Creek Pond; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species (in practice, rainbow trout is the only stocked species).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Clark County (Cold Creek Pond and Cold Creek)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Cold Creek Pond.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night at Cold Creek Pond, per NDOW's Clark County regulation table.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Clark County (Cold Creek Pond and Cold Creek)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night at Cold Creek Pond.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

stockingSchedule.note: NDOW's Cold Creek Pond Angler Information Guide states the pond is stocked in the spring and then again in the fall with Rainbow Trout.
